combinedDocument.title: Chainlink CRE Full Guide + conf-http-demo (TypeScript) — Complete LLM-Ready Reference (Feb 2026)
combinedDocument.version: 1.0 — February 18 2026 — fully updated with latest docs + conf-http-demo repo
combinedDocument.description: Single self-contained JSON combining the official Chainlink CRE Getting Started tutorial series (Overview + Part 1 TS) + the complete conf-http-demo repository. Perfect for any LLM code assistant (Cursor, Continue, Aider, Claude, etc.). Use this file to generate new confidential workflows, extend the demo, or learn CRE end-to-end.
combinedDocument.mainPurpose: Confidential HTTP workflows: vault-encoded secrets + enclave AES-GCM response encryption (never expose API keys or raw responses)

keyConcepts.CRE: Chainlink Runtime Environment — secure, verifiable offchain computation run by DONs in TEE enclaves
keyConcepts.Workflow: TypeScript/Go code compiled to WASM → executed in enclave
keyConcepts.ConfidentialHTTP: Secrets injected via {{.key}} templates from vaultDonSecrets; optional encryptOutput: true (AES-GCM inside enclave)
keyConcepts.Simulation: cre workflow simulate — full local test before deploy

gettingStartedOverview.whatYouWillBuildInOfficialTutorial: Onchain Calculator: Cron → HTTP API → EVM Read → compute → EVM Write
tutorialParts: Part 1: Project Setup & Simulation (covered in detail below)
prerequisitesFromDocs: CRE CLI installed + logged in (`cre login` after account at cre.chain.link)

cliSetupFromDocs.createProject: cre init → choose TypeScript → Helloworld template → name workflow

fullDemoFiles.project.yaml:
@1.0.0

staging-settings:
  rpcs:
    - chain-name: ethereum-testnet-sepolia
      url: https://ethereum-sepolia-rpc.publicnode.com

production-settings:
  rpcs:
    - chain-name: ethereum-testnet-sepolia
      url: https://ethereum-sepolia-rpc.publicnode.com
fullDemoFiles.my-workflow/workflow.yaml: # staging-settings + production-settings mapping to main.ts, config, secrets

confidentialHTTPExplanation.howItWorks:
1. Vault secret injection via {{.myApiKey}} in headers (never appears in plaintext code/config).
2. Request runs inside enclave.
3. Response body encrypted with AES-GCM using vault secret 'san_marino_aes_gcm_encryption_key'.
4. Only nonce + ciphertext+tag returned to workflow → printed as hex for easy browser decryption at https://www.ciphertools.org/tools/aes/gcm

keyParams.vaultDonSecrets: [{ key: "myApiKey", owner: config.owner }, { key: "san_marino_aes_gcm_encryption_key", owner: config.owner }]

confidentialHTTPExplanation.decryptionSteps:
1. Copy Nonce/IV hex
2. Copy Ciphertext+tag hex
3. Go to CipherTools AES-GCM
4. Decrypt + verify tag | 128-bit tag | 256-bit key = AES_KEY_ALL from .env

stepByStep: 2. Install CRE CLI (macOS/Linux/Windows guides in official docs)
8. Copy printed hex values → decrypt at CipherTools
setupInstructionsCombined.productionNotes: Set real owner in config.*.json for vaultDonSecrets. Never commit .env. Use production-settings for live DON deployment.

combinedDocument.howToUseThisJSONWithLLM: Copy entire JSON → paste in prompt: 'You are an expert Chainlink CRE developer. Here is the complete official getting started + full conf-http-demo as JSON: [paste] Now: 1. Explain how to add a POST request 2. Extend to write result onchain 3. Generate new workflow for weather API with encryption etc.' → perfect integration, zero hallucination.
